messages.x: For me I’d rather the opposition have some skin in the game. Think it suits us better this season.

Massive swing

Coventry - 1.03 to 1.06 to 1.06 to 1.13 to 1.11 to 1.10 to 1.14 to 1.30 to 1.53 to 1.36 to 1.11 to 1.04 to 1.04 to 1.06 to 1.02 to 1.00 to 1.00 to 1.00 to 1.00 to 1.00

Ipswich - 2.20 to 1.67 to 1.50 to 1.53 to 1.50 to 1.44 to 1.67 to 1.91 to 1.62 to 1.73 to 2.0 to 1.67 to 1.91 to 1.73 to 2.625 to 2.50 to 1.91 to 2.20 to 2.00 to 1.36

Boro - 2.50 to 3.25 to 3.75 to 2.75 to 2.50 to 2.20 to 1.83 to 1.50 to 1.33 to 1.40 to 1.50 to 2.0 to 1.67 to 1.80 to 1.44 to 1.80 to 2.00 to 1.80 to 2.63 to 3.50

Millwall - 13.0 to 5.50 to 9.0 to 9.5 to 4.0 to 9.5

Implied percentages….

Coventry - 97% to 94% to 94% to 88% to 90% to 91% to 88% to 77% to 65% to 73% to 90% to 96% to 96% to 94% to 99% to 100% to 100% to 100% to 100% to 100%

Ipswich - 45% to 60% to 67% to 65% to 67% to 69% to 60% to 52% to 62% to 58% to 50% to 60% to 52% to 58% to 38% to 40% to 52% to 45% to 50% to 73%

Boro - 40% to 31% to 27% to 36% to 40% to 45% to 55% to 67% to 75% to 71% to 71% to 50% to 60% to 56% to 69% to 56% to 50% to 56% to 38% to 29%

Millwall - 8% to 18% to 11% to 11% to 25% to 11%

BJ for Norwich or do we bring Davies back?
We will bring David back. I think we are more solid with BJ there, but Davis has the x-factor going forward. Especially when playing with Jaden
And Jaden comes on and scores the winner in the last 10
Did we call out Nunez enough for another great performance yesterday. The PPG when he plays….
Can someone else take the free kicks though?
Scores winner from Freekick next week 🙌

In fact for those who didn’t listen post match, he suggested we played Nunez yesterday instead of Saturday as he fears the occasion could be too much for him

I agree, I don’t think he is a particularly deep thinker. Plus, I cannot wait to hear those boos the first time he touches the ball
Saturday cannot come soon enough now. I’m so excited

Hindsight is wonderful, and moderating is hard as you’ve got guess the sentiment of everyone else, but one huge group makes a lot more sense. Ipswich chat, transfer chat and matchday chat is basically the same thing. Theres no need to police discussing a transfer in the wrong place if it’s now been 30 minutes since the transfer, a transfer is a transfer even if it happened half hour ago.
Wait until Mystic Luke and his 5000 twitter links to random Australian LBs "we are allegedly looking at"  makes his appearance as soon as the transfer window opens... 😬
He’ll hit the wall.
Or 2 teams are on the beach and 3 aren’t very good hence battling relegation? Seems to me it could be a lot harder teams they could be playing.
Correct, we feel the vast majority of posters here will self moderate. It’s meant to ebb and flow as a conversation might. All very easy going 🫶
Another monkey off our back, winning after going behind. My Noridge mate reckon we will be in for one hell of a game, so simple response, well it is your cup final for us it’s another game

Said a fair while back that I think if go 2ppg from end of August it would prob be enough - that would see us get to 87 and bar one of Boro or Millwall winning 5 out of 5 and a decent GD swing it will definitely be enough

As along as we just play the game on Saturday and not the occasion I think it’ll be a first Town win at Carrow Road for a long time. “Fair” play Joe you called second place at Christmas. Can’t see us being toppled now.
Only thing on the GD is that, if it does come into play, it will be because we have lost two games more than the other two, which might impact a bit with Boro.
Unlikely I know
Yeh, but 8 goals a big swing over a short period

I’m conflicted on that, as I also believe that not playing the occasion in terms of if they do, and it gives them an extra 5%, it could be a very tough day. You have to lean in to these occasions a bit.
I however will be leaning away from it until the day as I hate these fixtures 😂😂 will be leaning in to the Masters as a distraction. Come on Rosey!!!
Basically 4 each innit

Anybody mention Taylor’s booking yesterday? Thought that was hilarious. He’s obviously learning from Darnell….who was subtly at his shithouse best again. Brilliant moment in the 2nd when Furlong got the ball girl to throw the ball too far so he “had” to go and get it. He even told her where to throw it!! 🤣🤣🤣🤣

Just watched the highlights on Town TV, two things, Alan Judge is really good on co comms, really pleased we are getting him involved. Secondly it’s new information to me that Walton (very good again yesterday) put a good save onto the post! Didn’t see it properly up the other end.

So working with a nodge fan this morning unfortunately, he's is all over it being their cup final and their chance 'ruin' our top2 and for them to get into the playoffs on the baxk of us, too say he was both cocky and wetting himself would be an understatement!

I don’t see where he goes next. His career has fell off an absolute cliff

Cracking keeper!

Even putting aside the sheer banter of signing him from Norwich, and whilst he doesn't have flashy numbers, must be an argument that Marcelino Nunez is the signing of the summer in the EFL, for just how much he makes us tick, and our record with and without him in the side.

Nunez starts in the 10 role - 44 points from 18 games - 2.44ppg
Nunez doesn't start in the 10 role - 27 points from 20 games - 1.35ppg
Thought he stuck his foot in well too yesterday.
He’s my POTS I think. Easy vote could go to Philogene or Clarke but like Joe says the influence he has on the overall team performance is massive
Matisiwa for me but nunez a close second
Easy vote is Azor

Isn’t it great we have no clear and obvious choice as in previous seasons for POTS 

However, Nunez / Matusiwa are neck and neck for me currently
Turkey
Ben Johnson or gtfo
New teeth?

If you watched Nacho closely yesterday, there were so many little touches and short passes that then subsequently opened up the whole pitch. Two or three examples near the dugouts in the first half had KMK clapping furiously. And of course his involvement in Kasey’s goal. Sure, he was done after 60 minutes, but that was no surprise.
He just never takes that extra unnecessary touch and it knocks defenders of their spot before they are positioned right - moves the ball so nicely

Whereas Anis…
Matusiwa by a distance
Thought Anis looked fucked yesterday, easy to forget he’s been absolutely flogged this season

Very much disrupted us, CD had them aimed exactly at our weaknesses. Very impressive.
Sadly I can see that level of onslaught on Saturday from the off!! So much for us wanting teams to come at us 😬😂
Thought DOS and Greaves in fairness coped well. I liked Kipfre joining them. Also think BJ gets a vote.
We need to track their midfield. Especially the lad they replaced Nunez with.
Scored a belter versus Millwall
I was in the cock and pie for that! The pub erupted in cheer, it felt dirty
That was such a good team goal. Hate to say it, but they looked really good yesterday.
They are a good team and run themselves into the ground. It might go the distance and we need old school finishers in the final 20 to get us anything.
I think we will be fine on Saturday. It will be good for a team to come at us from the start, we will score on the counter

Against Millwall McKenna said they went man for man and we were sloppy with the ball. Yesterday I saw them pressing higher, but were they any other tactical changes, and should we be worried that we can't work these things out?
Yes. Whittlesford Parkway is a 15 minute walk from my front door, and then the train is direct, 90 minutes
I think a) we just got nervous, b) without Azor we did not hold the ball up as well. Nick has already said, the two in the middle were fantastic when chasing the game, but not at controlling it
I don’t think it matters how good you are, or how comfortable you have been, the fact that we had not come from behind was a monkey we had to get off our backs
Agreed but azor was there against Millwall, and we struggled to hold the ball
It probably takes some pressure off at the start of games now too. We know we can concede and it’s not game over
Azor isn't great on the ball in fairness. Noticeable how improved we looked at progressing the ball when he went off and Nunez moved deeper

Yes. That one was more tactical. Yesterday I think was more about game state and players on the pitch. We could have maybe made changes sooner, but actually, the period between 60 and 75 minutes felt the most comfortable
There was one ball out from Walton, that was a low skimmer to Akpom, that resulted in their attack and near post shot down our right. I’ve no idea what we were doing there
Fair point but I think in both games we have had a slice of luck in both teams failing to score and I'd love us to be able to manage to games better, even if we don't have majority possession.
Millwalls against the bar, yesterday the ball in or out of play……
Brum also brought on Osman and our right side got completely overloaded.

I think Akpom headed it straight back to their attacker didn’t he 🤣 the ball from Walton was pretty good Akpom
Just had a mare.

For two years, KM made pre arranged subs to give us extra legs and had a transformative approach. This season, we rarely improve with subs, they often struggle to make an impact and KM feels like he's also being forced into being more reactive too
On Akpom, was commented yesterday that he looked like he didn't know what to do. Think there is some truth in that, isn't sure whether to press high, drop deep, try and win flick ons etc.

My wife and I used to measure the speed of a pub in DPH drinks per hour. In my early thirties our local was 2 DPH, which is a fast pub, but as we moved out eight years later it had become a 1.5 DPH (your pint takes 45 mins to drink). The metric started to unravel when we realised that fun atmospheric pubs, problematic flat roof pubs and boring non descript pubs all had a high DPH for different reasons, but we still enjoy pre judging then measuring the DPH of a pub.

Since we’ve had kids most pubs now have a 0 DPH.
The Saints are in it..

Enjoyed this from SNLUK the other week

https://youtu.be/rY15PLImY3E?si=D_iTKEXa04JLnWOT

Was trying to workout why I feel so meh about Saturday. Think it’s cause everything about this season suggests that we’ll lose the derby, but still go up in second. It’s a season of relative consistency but just lacking moments, the real highs. Even Saturday: our first from behind win, yet there was little drama, just two quick fire first-half goals. The double over Coventry was great… but would have been all the better were we properly challenging them. The 5-0 over Sheff Utd was when they were shocking. Got turned over at Boro. First game of the season was a big occasion and we didn’t show up. Whole thing has felt like a slog. And yet… if we buck the trend and win in style on Saturday it’ll be all the sweeter. I still just don’t quite have the faith in this group, sadly.
Enjoyed this too! My partner is off on a girls Jolly up to Alicante tomorrow for the weekend and it was very apt for her 😬
Like this metric. Only question. Wouldn’t 1.5DPH mean your pint takes 40 minutes? 20 minutes per half pint.

Yeah think that is probably similar for a lot us. Still waiting on that moment or performance that really stands out.

Probably harsh on the squad but it’s how I feel too.
The answer is probably my atrocious maths but I couldn’t even tell you

I’m going for an Ipswich win Saturday and to remain second through to promotion. QPR will be a party for the Tractor Boys..

Agreed on the resilience, and we are going to certainly need it, I don’t think we’ve looked very good since Swansea truth be told. For us to get over the line we need to turn around something else and that’s our away form. Hopefully now Nunez is back that’s going to be the real difference maker, because if we play to our PPG when he’s started in the 10, we cant be caught.
